chunks.title: Catalog Description
chunks.content:
Production Systems Engineering. (3-0). Credit 3. Principles, models, and techniques
for planning and analysis of production and distribution systems; application of
linear, integer, and nonlinear optimization models and solution methods for
aggregate planning, supply chain planning, push (MRP) and pull (JIT) material flow
management, inventory control under deterministic and stochastic demands,
operations scheduling, and production scheduling. Prerequisites: Grade of C or
better in ISEN 230 and ISEN 320.

chunks.title: Course Prerequisites
chunks.content: Prerequisite/Corequisite(s): Grade of C or better in ISEN 230 and ISEN 320.

chunks.title: Course Learning Outcomes
chunks.content:
Upon completion of this course, the learner will be able to:

• describe different types and levels of decision making in a production system
• model, formulate, solve, analyze problems arising in aggregate production an
d supply chain planning
• model and analyze fundamental inventory control systems with certain/uncer
tain demand
• describe and analyze push (MRP) and pull production systems (JIT)
• model and solve deterministic operations scheduling problems
• model and solve basic project scheduling problems

chunks.title: Textbook and/or Resource Materials
chunks.content:
This material Is: Recommended
Production and Operations Analysis
ISBN: 978-1-4786-2306-9
Authors: Nahmias, S., Olsen T. N.
Publisher: Waveland Press
Publication Date: 2015
Edition: 8

chunks.title: Grading Policy
chunks.content:
Assignments (Home Works or other): 10%
Exams: 90%
There will be four exams:

• The first three exams are not comprehensive and will cover roughly one
third of course materials.
• At the end of the course, one optional comprehensive final exam will
be held.
• The lowest exam grade out of these four exams will be dropped, and the
overall course grade will be based on the four remaining exam grades at
30% weight each, towards the overall course grade.

Final grades will be assigned as follows:
A: [90,100] B: [80-90) C: [70,80) D: [50,70) F: [0,50)

chunks.title: Late Work Policy
chunks.content:
Work submitted by a student as makeup work for an excused absence is not
considered late work and is exempt from the late work policy (Student Rule 7).

chunks.title: Course Schedule
chunks.content:
Topical Outline and Exams Schedule

• Introduction to Production and Operations Strategy
• Forecasting
• Aggregate Production Planning
• Inventory Control: Known Demand
• Inventory Control: Uncertain Demand
• Material Requirements Planning (MRP) and Just-in-Time (JIT)
• Operations Scheduling
• Project Scheduling (time permitting)
